name: FitnessVR
short_description: Fitness VR is the ideal solution for mixing video games and sustained physical activities. Enter the Fitness VR Club as you go to the gym, and sweat your muscles to wake them up and strengthen them!
detailed_description:
FitnessVR is the first game in Virtual Reality to offer a unique sporting experience!

Choose your coach, intensity of your training and duration then start your rhythm training on the music.
Sessions can last from 5 to 45 minutes to suit all types of workouts, from beginners , to the more experienced .
You will be able to burn up to 250 calories in one session.
You can do these exercises without equipment or with bracelets of strength , to intensify the muscular effort.

You will perform ground exercises and upright , to work the muscles of the abdominals, thighs and glutes.
The exercises selected by your coach are simple and do not require special knowledge of fitness.

The activities are accessible to everyone , including seniors , and everyone goes at their own pace !

supported_languages:
English*, French*, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Arabic, Bulgarian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, Greek, Hungarian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese - Portugal, Portuguese - Brazil, Romanian, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Spanish - Latin America, Swedish, Thai, Traditional Chinese, Turkish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text:
[h1]Experienced on the Meta Quest 2[/h1]

You can view my gameplay & early impressions here: https://youtu.be/Gh-5npL_fy4

I don't think this app meets the minimum standards for a full release on Steam. It's barely functional, but it's full of bugs and issues. Honestly, if Steam did any type of Curation this is the exact type of game that would never be allowed to be on sale on the Steam store because it's currently not worth anything. 

It's supposed to be workout routine / exercise app for VR. It technically works. You can launch it and it runs. There's even some workout routines you can view. However, I experienced several bugs in my almost one hour of play total. Almost all of it was miserable due to these issues.

[b][u]Negatives:[/u][/b]
1) Avatar body not aligned correctly with my body, thus causing a blocked view
2) Avatar was about a foot off the ground (ground level didn't match room)
3) Workout routine skipping part of the program and ending suddenly
4) Laser pointers shooting out of my hand that I couldn't turn off
5) Settings not being saved upon exiting the game
6) No music? Is this a bug or a feature?
7) Part of the text being in French even though I chose English / mis-translation
8) App doesn't track your movements for results so you can just sit there and do nothing and get the same results as though you had tried to do them / no results screen
9) Have to register to get a custom avatar
10) Teleport only / snap turn only with left joystick on Meta Quest
11) Slight optimization issue (mostly ran 90 fps, but did drop all the way down to 60 fps at times)
 12) Makes you do floor exercises, but if you have motion controllers you'll probably break them trying to mimic the movements
13) Ugly UI & character models & repeated voice lines

[b][u]Positives:[/u][/b]
1) It didn't crash
2) If you look past all the negatives & take off your motion controllers & forget that you can do the same from a YouTube video for free, you can possibly get a good workout 

[b][u]Neutral:[/u][/b]
1) Runs on the Unity Engine
2) French developers / has French language support
3) I got this free from the Steam Curator Connect so at least I didn't waste my money on this (I did waste my time & effort though)

[b]Rate 2/10.[/b]

[quote]Please note: I received a free Steam key through the Steam Curator Connect program. You can join my Steam Curator group, Oculus Rift Reviews, here: https://steamcommunity.com/groups/Oculus_WMR [/quote]
it would not load past the loading screen for me no matter what i tried. was really looking forward to playing this
